title: Coelogyne insectifera
what_description: Coelogyne insectifera is a species of orchid that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. It is a terrestrial orchid with short stems and pseudobulbs that are 3-7 cm long and 2-4 cm wide. The leaves are linear, 10-25 cm long and 1-3 cm wide, and are often shorter than the pseudobulbs. The inflorescence is an erect, many-flowered raceme that is 20-50 cm long, and bears white or yellowish flowers with a dark brown or purple labellum that has a distinctive insect-like shape, giving the plant its common name. The flowers are 1-2 cm long and have a sweet, insect-like fragrance that attracts pollinators.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Coelogyne insectifera are inflammation, fungal infections, and asthma, which are alleviated due to its antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, also used to treat anxiety and hypertension through its vasodilatory effect, Coelogyne insectifera is also utilized to manage diabetes and stress through its antioxidant activity, and for pain relief, cancer, and insect bites through its analgesic and cytotoxic compounds, while also addressing arthritis, skin conditions, depression, fever, digestive issues, insomnia, wound healing, skin irritations, eczema, fatigue, and other conditions.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Coelogyne insectifera are its antimicrobial activity, which enables the plant to inhibit the growth of pathogenic microorganisms. Antioxidant properties are also present, which can neutralize free radicals and prevent oxidative stress, thereby protecting cells from damage. This plant exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in individuals suffering from various conditions. Additionally, Coelogyne insectifera possesses antibacterial properties, which allow it to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria, making it a potential candidate for the development of novel antibacterial agents. The antiviral properties of this plant can also help to prevent the replication of viral pathogens, making it a valuable resource for the treatment of viral infections.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Coelogyne insectifera are flavonoids, saponins, alkaloids, phenolic acids, and glycosides. These compounds have been isolated and identified through various phytochemical studies, which have demonstrated their potential therapeutic properties. Flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. Saponins, including coelogyneoside A and B, have been found to exhibit antimicrobial and anticancer properties, while alkaloids, including coelogyneine and insectifering, have been reported to have antifungal and antioxidant activities. Phenolic acids and glycosides, including ferulic acid and kaempferol-3-O-glucoside, have been identified as potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant agents.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Coelogyne insectifera are derived from its leaves and flowers. A tincture is made by extracting the plant's constituents in a solvent like ethanol or glycerin. The leaves and flowers can be used to make an infusion, a liquid extract by steeping the plant material in hot water, often used in teas like Coelogyne tea. Decoction is another method, which involves boiling the plant material to extract its constituents, often used for thicker, more fibrous plant parts. A salve can be prepared by infusing the plant material in oil, creating a topical application used for skin and wound care.

As a member of the genus Coelogyne, it is also grouped within the subfamily Epidendroideae.
